---
title: "PyApprox Tutorial Library"
subtitle: "Validated tutorials for uncertainty quantification"
---

## Getting Started

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Setup Environment](setup_environment.qmd) | Install PyApprox and verify your setup |
| [From Models to Decisions Under Uncertainty](models_decisions_uncertainty.qmd) | Why computational models need UQ, grounded in a composite cantilever beam |

## Forward UQ

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Forward Uncertainty Quantification](forward_uq.qmd) | The forward UQ problem: propagating input uncertainty through a model to characterize output uncertainty |
| [Random Variables and Distributions](random_variables_distributions.qmd) | Specifying parameter uncertainty with independent and copula-based distributions |
| [Monte Carlo Sampling](monte_carlo_sampling.qmd) | Estimating QoI statistics by random sampling: variability, sample size effects, and multi-output covariance |
| [Estimator Accuracy and MSE](estimator_accuracy_mse.qmd) | Bias, variance, and MSE of Monte Carlo estimators for scalar and vector-valued statistics |
| [Monte Carlo Budget Estimation](mc_budget_estimation.qmd) | Using pilot samples and the estimator API to predict the number of samples needed for a target accuracy |

## Multifidelity Estimation

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Control Variate Monte Carlo](control_variate_concept.qmd) | Reducing MC estimator variance using a correlated low-fidelity model with a known statistic |
| [Control Variate Analysis](control_variate_analysis.qmd) | Deriving the optimal control variate weight, variance reduction formula, and unbiasedness |
| [Control Variate Usage](control_variate_usage.qmd) | Using the PyApprox CVMC API to reduce estimator variance with a correlated low-fidelity model |
| [ACV Concept](acv_concept.qmd) | Approximate control variates: extending CVMC to multiple low-fidelity models without known statistics |
| [ACV Analysis](acv_analysis.qmd) | Deriving ACV variance reduction and comparing GMF, GIS, and GRD allocation strategies |
| [ACV Usage](acv_usage.qmd) | Using the PyApprox ACV API to allocate samples and estimate statistics with multiple models |
| [MFMC Concept](mfmc_concept.qmd) | Multi-fidelity Monte Carlo: exploiting a hierarchy of model fidelities for variance reduction |
| [MFMC Analysis](mfmc_analysis.qmd) | Optimal sample ratios, variance reduction formulas, and numerical verification of MFMC and MLMC |
| [MFMC Usage](mfmc_usage.qmd) | Using the PyApprox MFMC and MLMC API to allocate samples, visualize allocations, and verify estimates |
| [Multi-Level Monte Carlo](mlmc_concept.qmd) | Estimating high-fidelity model statistics using a hierarchy of models by accumulating corrections between consecutive levels |
| [MLMC Analysis](mlmc_analysis.qmd) | Deriving the optimal MLMC sample allocation, minimum achievable cost, and the MLMC complexity theorem |
| [MLMC Usage](mlmc_usage.qmd) | Using the PyApprox MLMC API to set up a model hierarchy, allocate samples optimally, and compare variance reduction against MC |
| [General ACV Concept](acv_many_models_concept.qmd) | How using all LF models as direct control variates breaks the CV-1 variance ceiling |
| [General ACV Analysis](acv_many_models_analysis.qmd) | Deriving the optimal weight matrix and minimum covariance via Schur complement |
| [ACV Allocation Matrices](acv_allocation_matrices.qmd) | How allocation matrices encode sample-set structure for all ACV estimators |
| [ACV Covariances](acv_covariances.qmd) | Explicit formulas for the covariance blocks that determine ACV estimator variance |
| [Multi-Output ACV Concept](multioutput_acv_concept.qmd) | Why estimating several QoIs jointly with multi-fidelity corrections improves each individual estimate |
| [Multi-Output ACV Analysis](multioutput_acv_analysis.qmd) | Deriving the MOACV optimal weight matrix and the condition under which joint estimation beats independent scalar ACVs |
| [Multi-Output ACV Usage](multioutput_acv_usage.qmd) | Running MOACV and SOACV end-to-end, comparing variance reduction, and choosing between joint and per-QoI strategies |
| [PACV Concept](pacv_concept.qmd) | How a single recursion index unifies MLMC, MFMC, ACVMF, and ACVIS as special cases of one family |
| [PACV Analysis](pacv_analysis.qmd) | Formal definition of GMF, GRD, GIS families and the covariance formulas enabling enumeration-based best-estimator selection |
| [PACV Usage](pacv_usage.qmd) | Configuring PACV estimators, enumerating all valid structures, and selecting the best estimator for a given budget |

## Inverse UQ

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Introduction to Bayesian Inference](bayesian_inference_intro.qmd) | From data to parameters: updating beliefs using Bayes' theorem |
| [Sampling the Posterior with MCMC](mcmc_sampling.qmd) | Using Markov chain Monte Carlo to generate posterior samples when grid evaluation is infeasible |
| [Practical MCMC: MH and DRAM](dram_mcmc.qmd) | Tuning proposal distributions, adaptive methods, and convergence diagnostics for MCMC |

## Experimental Design

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Introduction to Experimental Design](experimental_design_intro.qmd) | Why sensor placement matters: comparing designs by posterior covariance and Expected Information Gain |

## Bayesian OED

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Bayesian OED: KL Concept](boed_kl_concept.qmd) | What Expected Information Gain measures and why it is the right objective for choosing experiments |
| [Bayesian OED: Double-Loop Estimator](boed_kl_estimator.qmd) | How the double-loop MC estimator approximates EIG by treating the marginal evidence as a nested average |
| [Bayesian OED: Gradients](boed_kl_gradients.qmd) | The reparameterization trick, the C1+C2+C3 gradient decomposition, and the corrected Hessian |
| [Bayesian OED: Convergence](boed_kl_usage.qmd) | Using KLOEDDiagnostics to measure MSE, decompose bias and variance, and confirm convergence |
| [Bayesian OED: MC vs Halton](boed_kl_qmc.qmd) | Why randomly-shifted Halton sequences reduce MSE compared to i.i.d. MC for the double-loop EIG estimator |
| [Bayesian OED: Nonlinear Usage](boed_kl_nonlinear_usage.qmd) | Applying EIG-based OED to a Lotka-Volterra predator-prey system — a nonlinear model with no analytic EIG |
| [Bayesian OED: Design Stability](boed_kl_design_stability.qmd) | Demonstrating that OED designs depend on MC realizations at fixed budget and stabilize as budget grows |
| [Goal-Oriented OED: Concept](boed_pred_concept.qmd) | Why targeting prediction uncertainty rather than parameter uncertainty changes which experiments are optimal |
| [Goal-Oriented OED: Gaussian Analysis](boed_pred_gaussian_analysis.qmd) | Closed-form posterior mean distribution, entropic risk deviation, and AVaR deviation for Gaussian push-forwards |
| [Goal-Oriented OED: Log-Normal Analysis](boed_pred_lognormal_analysis.qmd) | Deriving the distribution and expected AVaR deviation of the standard deviation of a log-normal QoI |
| [Goal-Oriented OED: Convergence](boed_pred_usage.qmd) | Verifying numerical risk-aware utility estimates converge to analytical values with MC vs. Halton |
| [Goal-Oriented OED: Nonlinear Usage](boed_pred_nonlinear_usage.qmd) | Comparing std-deviation and entropic deviation objectives for goal-oriented OED on Lotka-Volterra |
| [OED Data Workflow](boed_data_workflow_usage.qmd) | Generating, saving, and reusing expensive model evaluations so OED optimisation avoids re-running the forward model |
| [Surrogate-Accelerated OED](boed_surrogate_usage.qmd) | Building a sparse-grid surrogate to enable large-sample-budget OED infeasible with the original model |

## Surrogate Modeling

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Building a Polynomial Chaos Surrogate](polynomial_chaos_surrogates.qmd) | Approximate an expensive model with a polynomial chaos expansion |
| [Overfitting and Cross-Validation](practical_pce_fitting.qmd) | Choose the polynomial degree using LOO cross-validation |
| [Sparse Polynomial Approximation](sparse_pce_fitting.qmd) | OMP and BPDN/LASSO for high-dimensional polynomial chaos |
| [UQ with Polynomial Chaos](uq_with_pce.qmd) | Extract analytical moments, marginal densities, and convergence comparisons from a fitted PCE |
| [Function Train Surrogates](function_train_surrogates.qmd) | Build intuition for low-rank functions and the function train decomposition |
| [UQ with Function Trains](uq_with_functiontrain.qmd) | Extract analytical moments, marginal densities, and input marginals from a fitted function train |

## Sparse Grid Interpolation

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Piecewise Polynomial Quadrature](piecewise_quadrature.qmd) | Rectangle, trapezoid, and Simpson's rules with error derivations |
| [Gauss Quadrature](gauss_quadrature.qmd) | Optimal nodes from orthogonal polynomials with exponential convergence |
| [Lagrange Interpolation](lagrange_interpolation.qmd) | Lagrange basis polynomials, Runge phenomenon, and optimal node placement |
| [Leja Sequences](leja_sequences.qmd) | Nested points for adaptive interpolation via greedy determinant maximisation |
| [Piecewise Interpolation](piecewise_interpolation.qmd) | Local basis functions for smooth and discontinuous functions |
| [Tensor Product Interpolation](tensor_product_interpolation.qmd) | Multivariate approximation via Cartesian products of 1D bases |
| [Isotropic Sparse Grids](isotropic_sparse_grids.qmd) | Smolyak combination technique for breaking the curse of dimensionality |
| [Adaptive Sparse Grids](adaptive_sparse_grids.qmd) | Data-driven refinement for anisotropic functions |
| [Multifidelity Sparse Grids](multifidelity_sparse_grids.qmd) | Multi-index collocation for model hierarchies with varying cost and accuracy |
| [Sparse Grid to PCE Conversion](sparse_grid_to_pce_conversion.qmd) | Spectral projection converting Lagrange-based sparse grid interpolants into Polynomial Chaos Expansions |
| [UQ with Sparse Grids](uq_with_sparse_grids.qmd) | Compute moments from sparse grid quadrature, convert to PCE for Sobol indices, and compare against MC |

## Gaussian Process Surrogates

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Building a GP Surrogate](gp_surrogate.qmd) | Approximate an expensive model with a Gaussian process and quantify prediction uncertainty |
| [UQ with Gaussian Processes](uq_with_gp.qmd) | Compute analytical moments, marginal densities, and main effects from a fitted GP — without additional model evaluations |
| [Adaptive GP Sampling](adaptive_gp_sampling.qmd) | Choose training points that maximally reduce GP posterior uncertainty using greedy Cholesky and IVAR strategies |

## Sensitivity Analysis

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Sensitivity Analysis Concept](sensitivity_analysis_concept.qmd) | Variance decomposition and Sobol indices: identifying which uncertain inputs drive output variability |
| [Sobol Sensitivity Analysis](sobol_sensitivity_analysis.qmd) | Variance-based sensitivity indices for identifying important parameters |
| [PCE-Based Sensitivity](pce_sensitivity.qmd) | Computing Sobol indices analytically from polynomial chaos expansion coefficients |
| [GP-Based Sensitivity](gp_sensitivity.qmd) | Computing Sobol indices analytically from GP kernel integrals and verifying against fix-and-freeze estimates |
| [Morris Screening](morris_screening.qmd) | One-at-a-time elementary effects for cheap parameter screening |
| [Bin-Based Sensitivity](bin_based_sensitivity.qmd) | Variance-based sensitivity indices via binning estimation |
| [FT Sensitivity: Math](functiontrain_sensitivity_math.qmd) | Deriving Sobol indices analytically from PCE FunctionTrain structure |
| [FT Sensitivity: Usage](functiontrain_sensitivity.qmd) | Computing Sobol indices analytically with FunctionTrainSensitivity |

## Optimization Under Uncertainty

| Tutorial | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Design Under Uncertainty](design_under_uncertainty.qmd) | Optimizing system design when model inputs are uncertain |
